mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investor sentiment improves.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month RSAFS growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a significant weakening of the labor market, which typically precedes a broader economic slowdown and a decline in corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate crosses a high threshold, it indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle by the central bank. This increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with a persistently low Federal Funds Rate signals an accommodative economic environment where growth is robust without immediate monetary tightening concerns. This boosts investor confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Periods characterized by GDPC1 growth above 2% annualized and FEDFUNDS below 1% do not show significantly higher SPX 20-day returns compared to other periods.

mechanism_narrative: Robust job growth (PAYEMS) coupled with a sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) suggests a potential productivity slowdown or supply-side constraints. This scenario can lead to stagflationary concerns, negatively impacting corporate profitability and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3mo avg growth > 0.1% AND INDPRO 3mo avg change < -0.1%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where PAYEMS shows strong growth and INDPRO is simultaneously declining.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a rising CPI signals persistent price pressures, forcing the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance. This increases the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In high inflation regimes (CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3%), SPX 20-day forward returns are not negatively correlated with month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, strong housing starts indicate robust consumer and investment demand, signaling economic health and driving positive S&P 500 returns. This effect is amplified by cheap credit, encouraging construction and related spending.
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a key economic driver
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1% and HOUST is above 1200k units, SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive on average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion. This directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investors anticipate continued profitability.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month INDPRO growth over any 5-year rolling window.
